key_claim: Training generative models on model-generated content causes irreversible Model Collapse where tails of the original distribution disappear.
methodology: Theoretical analysis and empirical demonstrations of model collapse in Variational Autoencoders, Gaussian Mixture Models, and LLMs trained on recursively generated data.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how model collapse specifically affects social-reasoning capabilities or whether provenance-aware data curation (e.g., L_auth) could mitigate tail loss.
theoretical_framework: Model Collapse theory describing irreversible tail disappearance in iteratively trained generative models

key_claim: AI-generated synthetic medical data causes vocabulary collapse of ~98.9%, vanishing rare findings, and tripling of false reassurance rates to 40%.
methodology: Analysis of 800K+ synthetic medical data points measuring vocabulary diversity, rare-finding preservation, false reassurance rates, and diagnostic confidence.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether provenance-aware data collection or authenticity-tracking frameworks could preserve rare clinical findings and prevent domain-specific tail loss.
theoretical_framework: Domain-specific model collapse / tail-loss framework applied to clinical data

key_claim: Treating synthetic data as negative guidance during generation can prevent model autophagy disorder and maintain quality and diversity.
methodology: SIMS (Self-IMproving diffusion models with Synthetic data) uses self-synthesized data to provide negative guidance during the generative process, steering away from the non-ideal synthetic data manifold.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether negative-guidance approaches transfer to language model social reasoning or whether data provenance tracking could complement or replace synthetic-data filtering.
theoretical_framework: Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD) framework with negative guidance mitigation

key_claim: Introducing an idealized correction function makes self-consuming generative training loops exponentially more stable against collapse.
methodology: Theoretical analysis of self-consuming loops with correction functions, plus empirical validation using self-correction functions based on expert knowledge (e.g., physics simulators) to approximate idealized correctors at scale.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether correction functions can preserve socially nuanced or culturally authentic training signals, nor whether data authenticity provenance serves as a complementary stabilization mechanism.
theoretical_framework: Idealized correction function framework for stabilizing self-consuming generative loops

key_claim: Indiscriminate use of model-generated content in training causes irreversible model collapse where tails of the original data distribution disappear.
methodology: Theoretical analysis and empirical demonstrations of model collapse using LLMs (GPT-2), variational autoencoders (VAEs), and Gaussian mixture models (GMMs) trained on recursively generated data.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether provenance-aware data curation or authenticity verification of training data could prevent or mitigate model collapse.
theoretical_framework: Model collapse theory describing how iterative training on synthetic data leads to irreversible loss of distributional tails

key_claim: Data curation in self-consuming generative models can provably optimize human preferences and prevent model collapse under iterative retraining.
methodology: Theoretical analysis of iterated retraining of generative models with curated (human-feedback-filtered) synthetic data, studying conditions for stability versus collapse.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how provenance metadata or authenticity labels (real vs. synthetic) could be used to operationalize curation at web scale.
theoretical_framework: Formal framework for self-consuming generative models with data curation analyzing convergence and human preference optimization

key_claim: Reversing gradient updates from self-training on synthetic data extrapolates away from degraded weights and improves image generation quality.
methodology: Neon fine-tunes a base model on its own synthesized data then reverses gradient updates to extrapolate away from degraded weights, with theoretical proof that high-probability sampling creates predictable anti-alignment bias.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether ensuring training data authenticity or provenance tracking could be a complementary or alternative strategy to mitigate model collapse.
theoretical_framework: Neon (Negative Extrapolation frOm self-traiNing) with theoretical analysis of anti-alignment bias from high-probability samplers

key_claim: Model collapse can be characterized directly from next-token probability distributions using surplexity, without requiring knowledge of whether data is human- or AI-generated.
methodology: Introduces surplexity and related measures computed from next-token probability distributions to characterize and detect model collapse, with experiments varying training set complexity and autophagy extent.
limitation: Current mitigation methods assume reliable knowledge of whether training data is human-authored or AI-generated, and existing characterizations of collapse remain limited.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how provenance-aware data collection (e.g., authenticity labels) could be integrated with surplexity-based detection to prevent collapse in practice.
theoretical_framework: Surplexity: measures derived from next-token probability distributions to characterize model collapse

key_claim: Up to 22.5% of computer science papers showed LLM modification by September 2024, demonstrating academic text contamination at scale.
methodology: Population-level analysis of 1.1 million scientific papers to detect LLM-modified content using statistical indicators.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether LLM contamination of scientific literature degrades the quality of post-training data sourced from academic corpora and its downstream effects on social reasoning.

key_claim: 74.2% of new English webpages contain some AI content, but only 2.5% are purely AI-generated.
methodology: Analysis of 900K English webpages sampled in April 2025 to classify AI content presence and purity levels.
what_it_does_NOT_address: How the distinction between partial and pure AI content affects provenance-aware data collection strategies like those in CampusGo or authenticity-filtered training pipelines.

key_claim: AI-generated articles surpassed human-written articles in raw web volume around November 2024, but 86% of Google-ranking articles remain human-written.
methodology: Analysis of 65K URLs from Common Crawl to estimate the proportion of AI-generated versus human-written articles over time.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether quality-filtering mechanisms (e.g., FineWeb/RefinedWeb-style curation) sufficiently preserve data authenticity to maintain social-reasoning capabilities in post-training.

key_claim: 67% pool contamination with AI-generated content leads to over 80% exposure contamination in retrieval systems, creating a homogenized yet deceptively healthy state.
methodology: Controlled experiments simulating SEO-style and adversarial AI content contamination in search and RAG retrieval pipelines, comparing BM25 and LLM-based rankers.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether retrieval collapse affects the authenticity of post-training data pipelines and downstream social-reasoning performance of LLMs trained on contaminated retrieved evidence.
dataset_or_benchmark: Controlled synthetic contamination pools (SEO and adversarial scenarios)
theoretical_framework: Retrieval Collapse — a two-stage ecosystem-level failure mode where AI content dominates search results eroding source diversity, then low-quality or adversarial content infiltrates the retrieval pipeline

key_claim: AI-generated content is highly prevalent in MOOC student responses and can be identified through textual analysis metrics at scale.
methodology: Large-scale textual analysis of over 4045 student responses from an introductory IoT MOOC using various metrics to distinguish AI-generated from human-generated content.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether the provenance or authenticity of training data used by LLMs influences the quality or detectability of AI-generated student responses.
dataset_or_benchmark: 4045 student responses from an introductory IoT MOOC

key_claim: AI-generated web content causes Retrieval Collapse where 67% pool contamination leads to over 80% exposure contamination in search results, homogenizing sources while masking quality degradation.
methodology: Controlled experiments with SEO-style and adversarially crafted AI-generated content to measure contamination propagation in retrieval pipelines using BM25 and LLM-based rankers.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection could mitigate retrieval collapse caused by synthetic content pollution.

key_claim: AI-generated hyperrealistic wildlife images and videos threaten conservation by distorting public perceptions of species through social media, anthropomorphism, and nature disconnection.
methodology: Conceptual analysis identifying three societal characteristics (social media influence, anthropomorphism, nature disconnection) that amplify risks of AI-generated wildlife media.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data provenance or authenticity labeling of AI-generated wildlife content could mitigate downstream perceptual distortions.

key_claim: A fine-tuned Turkish BERT classifier achieves 0.9708 F1 score for detecting AI-rewritten content in Turkish news media with temporally stable performance across 2023–2026.
methodology: Fine-tuning dbmdz/bert-base-turkish-cased on 3,600 labeled articles from three Turkish outlets for binary AI-rewritten content classification, then deploying on 3,500+ unseen articles.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether provenance metadata or authentic sourcing of training data affects the LLMs that generate the news content being detected.

key_claim: A supervised detection framework can identify unmarked AI-generated content in leading Russian online news media, revealing measurable prevalence across 20 top outlets.
methodology: Supervised binary text classification applied to a large corpus of news texts from 20 leading Russian online media outlets collected March–May 2025, selected via SimilarWeb traffic rankings.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how data provenance or authenticity of training corpora influences the social-reasoning capabilities of the LLMs generating the detected content.

key_claim: The Dead Internet Theory is substantiated by the growing dominance of bots and AI-generated content that fundamentally alters the structure of online interactions.
methodology: Critical review and investigation of the Dead Internet Theory through analysis of AI-driven models (e.g., GPT-3) and bot prevalence data (e.g., Imperva reports).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection could mitigate the degradation of online content quality.

key_claim: Approximately 12% of shared images in 2024 U.S. election discourse on X are AI-generated, with 10% of users responsible for 80% of their dissemination.
methodology: Large-scale analysis of Twitter/X images related to the 2024 U.S. Presidential Election using AI-generated image detection, characterizing superspreaders and emotional reception.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether provenance-aware data collection or training-data authenticity affects the social reasoning or persuasive impact of AI-generated political content.

key_claim: Datasets contaminated with synthetic images degrade online continual learning performance, and entropy-based selection can mitigate this effect.
methodology: Experimental evaluation of online continual learning methods on datasets contaminated with diffusion-generated synthetic images; proposed Entropy Selection with Real-synthetic discrimination.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how provenance metadata or authenticity-aware filtering (e.g., L_auth) could systematically prevent synthetic contamination during large-scale data collection.

key_claim: AI-generated labels on social media posts induce a small negative affective shift ('emotional tax') on true content, while cognitive effects remain stable over 18 days.
methodology: Two-wave online experiment (N=400, N=256; 18-day interval) with health posts varying in veracity and content type, all carrying AI-generated labels; cross-wave models assessed temporal continuity.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how data provenance or authenticity of training data influences the social-reasoning quality of the AI systems generating labeled content.

key_claim: AI-generated labels on social media posts induce a small negative affective shift ('emotional tax') on true content, while cognitive effects remain stable over 18 days.
methodology: Two-wave online experiment (N=400, N=256; 18-day interval) with health posts varying in veracity and content type, all carrying AI-generated labels; cross-wave models assessed temporal continuity.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how data provenance or authenticity of training data influences the social-reasoning quality of the AI systems generating labeled content.

key_claim: People across 27 European countries perceive AI-generated and human-generated fake news headlines about the Russo-Ukrainian war similarly in veracity and sharing intentions.
methodology: Preregistered experiment embedded in a large-scale web survey across 27 European countries; participants evaluated eight news headlines (AI- vs. human-generated, real vs. fake) on perceived veracity and sharing intention.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether provenance-aware post-training data collection could improve AI models' social reasoning or reduce generation of persuasive misinformation.

key_claim: ALBERT, IndicBERT, and SVM models can effectively detect AI-generated product reviews in Malayalam and Tamil.
methodology: Classification of AI-generated vs. human-written product reviews in Dravidian languages using ALBERT, IndicBERT, and SVM models.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not explore how the authenticity or provenance of training data affects model social-reasoning capabilities or generalization beyond review detection.
dataset_or_benchmark: DravidianLangTech 2025 shared task dataset (AI-generated product reviews in Malayalam and Tamil)

key_claim: The arrival of AI-generated content on Pixiv has measurably impacted the social media ecosystem including community dynamics and content engagement patterns.
methodology: Large-scale observational analysis of 15.2 million posts on Pixiv, comparing human-created and AI-generated content across engagement and community metrics.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how the provenance or authenticity of training data behind AIGC affects the social reasoning capabilities of the models producing that content.

key_claim: A watermark embedded via green-token promotion during LLM sampling is algorithmically detectable from short token spans with negligible impact on text quality.
methodology: Watermarking framework that selects randomized green token sets before generation and softly promotes them during sampling; detection via a statistical hypothesis test with interpretable p-values; information-theoretic sensitivity analysis.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether watermarking can verify the authenticity or provenance of post-training data used to fine-tune models, only marks generated output.
theoretical_framework: Information-theoretic framework for watermark sensitivity analysis

key_claim: Paraphrasing with an 11B-parameter model (DIPPER) reduces DetectGPT detection accuracy from 70.3% to 4.6% at 1% false positive rate, but retrieval-based defenses can restore robustness.
methodology: Built DIPPER, an 11B parameter paraphrase model conditioned on context with controllable lexical diversity and reordering; stress-tested watermarking, GPTZero, DetectGPT, and OpenAI's classifier; proposed retrieval-based defense.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data provenance or authenticity of post-training corpora influences the detectability or social-reasoning quality of generated text.

key_claim: Targeted high-perplexity masking paired with CodeBERT infilling effectively detects AI-generated code assignments, outperforming random masking approaches.
methodology: Targeted masking perturbation that preferentially masks high-perplexity code regions, followed by fine-tuned CodeBERT infilling and comprehensive scoring to distinguish AI-generated from human-written code.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether the provenance or authenticity of the data used to train code-generating LLMs affects the detectability or reasoning quality of generated outputs.

key_claim: Hybrid detection approaches combining multiple modalities achieve up to 92% accuracy in identifying synthetic content, outperforming single-modality methods.
methodology: Systematic review of detection approaches for synthetic content across text, image, audio, and video modalities, including stylometric analysis, watermarking, pixel prediction, dual-stream networks, ML models, blockchain, and hybrid methods.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance or authenticity of training data affects the quality of social reasoning in language models.

key_claim: Detectability of AI-generated text depends on a combination of factors including generation method, domain, and detection technique, making no single approach universally reliable.
methodology: Survey synthesizing state-of-the-art AIGT detection approaches including watermarking, statistical and stylistic analysis, and machine learning classification, alongside existing datasets.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how provenance-aware data collection or training data authenticity affects downstream model capabilities such as social reasoning.

key_claim: Recursively training language models on synthetic text produced by predecessors leads to a consistent decline in lexical, syntactic, and semantic diversity, especially for creative tasks.
methodology: Recursive finetuning experiments across multiple NLG tasks in English, evaluated with novel and adapted metrics for lexical, syntactic, and semantic diversity.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the authenticity or provenance of training data specifically affects social-reasoning capabilities, nor does it explore inference-time scaling as a compensating mechanism.

key_claim: The proposed (α-Precision, β-Recall, Authenticity) metric suite enables sample-level evaluation of generative models, where Authenticity quantifies whether generated samples are novel versus memorized from training data.
methodology: Formal definition and derivation of three complementary metrics (α-Precision, β-Recall, Authenticity) for evaluating fidelity, coverage, and novelty of generative model outputs at the sample level.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how training data provenance predicts downstream model capability (e.g., social reasoning); its notion of authenticity concerns sample-level fidelity/novelty rather than data provenance effects on post-training outcomes.
theoretical_framework: (α-Precision, β-Recall, Authenticity) framework for generative model evaluation

key_claim: Bangla literary corpora exhibit significantly higher lexical richness and structural variation than newspaper corpora despite smaller size, as shown by multiple diversity metrics.
methodology: Corpus-driven statistical analysis comparing type-token ratio, hapax legomena ratio, bigram diversity, syllable/word lengths, Zipf's Law adherence, n-gram perplexity across literary and newspaper corpora.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how corpus-level lexical diversity differences affect downstream NLP model social-reasoning performance or how data provenance influences post-training outcomes.

key_claim: An empirical functional relation between word entropy and type-token ratio exists across corpora and languages, analytically derivable from Zipf's and Heaps' laws in the large-text limit.
methodology: Quantitative analysis of entropy and type-token ratio across six gigaword corpora in three languages, deriving an analytical expression from Zipf's and Heaps' laws and validating it empirically.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how lexical diversity metrics relate to data authenticity or provenance-aware corpus construction for improving social reasoning in language models.
dataset_or_benchmark: gigaword corpora of books, news articles, and tweets in English, Spanish, and Turkish

key_claim: An empirical functional relation between word entropy and type-token ratio exists across corpora and languages, analytically derivable from Zipf's and Heaps' laws in the large-text limit.
methodology: Quantitative analysis of entropy and type-token ratio across six gigaword corpora in three languages, deriving an analytical expression from Zipf's and Heaps' laws and validating it empirically.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not explore whether diversity metrics of training corpora predict downstream model behavior, particularly in social reasoning or provenance-sensitive settings.
dataset_or_benchmark: gigaword corpora of books, news articles, and tweets in English, Spanish, and Turkish

key_claim: Ratio-matching via denoising cross-entropy in discrete diffusion language models outperforms score-entropy methods with up to 10% lower perplexity and 15% faster training.
methodology: Derivation of three new KL divergence theorems for discrete CTMC diffusion models yielding an improved perplexity upper bound, combined with empirical comparison of denoising cross-entropy vs. score-entropy training objectives.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection affects social-reasoning capabilities of diffusion-based language models.
theoretical_framework: Continuous-time discrete Markov chain (CTMC) framework for discrete diffusion

key_claim: An NLP-based approach can distinguish AI-generated phishing messages from both legitimate emails and human-written phishing attempts.
methodology: Assembled a balanced dataset of authentic emails, human-written phishing samples, and synthetic AI-generated phishing messages; applied NLP-based classification to separate the three categories.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how provenance metadata of training data influences LLM social-reasoning capabilities or the authenticity of generated communication.
dataset_or_benchmark: custom dataset of authentic emails, human-written phishing, and AI-generated phishing

key_claim: Traditional corpus-based complexity indices (TTR, word-level Entropy) are less robust to corpus size and content variation than newer indices (Word Information Density, Lexical Diversity) across 47 languages.
methodology: Systematic robustness testing of four complexity indices (TTR, word-level Entropy, Word Information Density, Lexical Diversity) estimated from raw texts across 47 languages, varying corpus size and content.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection affects language model social reasoning.
theoretical_framework: Cross-linguistic trade-off between word-internal and across-word distributional complexity

key_claim: Adaptively watermarking only high-entropy token distributions and scaling logits based on semantic embeddings improves watermarked text quality, security, and robustness.
methodology: Adaptive watermarking strategy that selectively modifies high-entropy token distributions using an auxiliary model and scales output logits proportionally based on semantic embeddings of preceding tokens instead of fixed green/red lists.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data provenance or post-training data authenticity impacts social-reasoning outcomes in LLMs.

key_claim: Recursive self-training acts as a sharpening operator on the data distribution, causing systematic tail collapse and loss of structural diversity, formalized via a tail invariance principle.
methodology: Formalized dataset degradation as an iterative distributional transformation on the loss distribution; supported by controlled experiments on discrete distributions, continuous models, and language models.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether provenance-aware data collection (e.g., L_auth or CampusGo) can mitigate tail collapse, nor how inference-time scaling may compensate for distribution sharpening.
theoretical_framework: Tail invariance principle and loss distribution collapse framework

key_claim: Recursive self-training acts as a sharpening operator on the data distribution, causing systematic loss of tail probability mass and structural diversity collapse.
methodology: Formalization of recursive self-training as an iterative distributional transformation; controlled experiments on discrete distributions, continuous models, and language models.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether provenance-aware data collection (e.g., authentic human-generated data) can counteract the tail collapse mechanism, nor how inference-time scaling interacts with distributional sharpening.
theoretical_framework: Loss distribution collapse theory with a tail invariance principle

key_claim: Lexical diversity in Czech L2 texts increases with writer proficiency, with significant differences between Slavic and non-Slavic native speaker groups.
methodology: Quantitative analysis of lexical diversity using Moving Average Type-Token Ratio (MATTR) across CEFR proficiency levels (A1–C1).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data authenticity or provenance of learner texts affects downstream model training or social-reasoning evaluations.

key_claim: CurveMark combines probability curvature analysis with dynamic semantic watermarking to improve AI-generated text detection without requiring prior knowledge of the source model.
methodology: Dual-channel detection framework using information-theoretic probability curvature analysis and entropy-aware semantic watermarking, with Bayesian multi-hypothesis detection for model-agnostic inference.
limitation: Current methods suffer from limited information capture, poor rate-distortion trade-offs, and vulnerability to adversarial perturbations; CurveMark addresses these but specific residual limitations are not stated.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how detection of AI-generated text relates to post-training data authenticity or its downstream effects on social reasoning in LLMs.
theoretical_framework: Information-theoretic framework maximizing mutual information between text sources and observable features

key_claim: Source language properties of machine-translated training data shape model behavior: lexical diversity drives general perplexity while source-target syntactic similarity drives grammatical performance.
methodology: Training small English language models on English text machine-translated from 24 typologically diverse source languages; systematic analysis of how source language and corpus properties influence acceptability judgments and language modeling.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether using authentic (non-translated) human-generated data versus machine-translated data differentially affects social-reasoning capabilities, nor the role of data provenance tracking in mitigating translationese effects.

key_claim: Integral probability metrics with bounded test classes can be evaluated from finite samples up to multiplicative and additive approximation errors.
methodology: Theoretical analysis of evaluability for generative model metrics, studying IPMs and Rényi divergences under finite sample conditions.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether data authenticity or provenance of training data affects the evaluability or reliability of these generative model metrics.
theoretical_framework: Statistical evaluability framework for generative models based on IPMs and Rényi divergences

key_claim: LLM-prompted authorship impersonation texts fail to evade existing forensic authorship verification systems.
methodology: Generated impersonation texts using GPT-4o under four prompting conditions across three genres, then evaluated against non-neural (n-gram tracing, RBI, LambdaG) and neural (AdHominem, LUAR, STAR) AV methods within a likelihood-ratio framework.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware fine-tuning could improve LLMs' ability to produce more convincing impersonations or enhance verification robustness.

key_claim: Augmenting reverse KL with forward KL during high teacher entropy in on-policy distillation maintains generation diversity and improves student-teacher alignment.
methodology: Entropy-aware on-policy distillation that dynamically switches between reverse KL and forward KL based on teacher entropy, evaluated on language model distillation tasks.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the authenticity or provenance of the training data used for the teacher model affects the quality of distilled social reasoning.
theoretical_framework: Entropy-aware combination of forward and reverse KL divergence objectives

key_claim: Symmetrically aligning intermediate thought processes via Logit Lens projections consistently outperforms standard knowledge distillation of LLMs.
methodology: Projects intermediate hidden states into vocabulary space using Logit Lens and enforces structural alignment via a symmetric divergence objective during knowledge distillation.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not consider how post-training data authenticity or provenance influences the fidelity of distilled representations for social reasoning tasks.
theoretical_framework: DistillLens framework using Logit Lens projection with symmetric divergence alignment

key_claim: Advantage Re-weighting Mechanism (ARM) mitigates mode collapse in RLVR by equilibrating confidence across correct responses, redistributing probability mass toward under-explored reasoning paths.
methodology: Proposes ARM that incorporates Prompt Perplexity and Answer Confidence into advantage estimation to dynamically reshape reward signals in GRPO-based policy optimization for LLM reasoning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection influences the diversity and quality of reasoning outcomes.
theoretical_framework: Advantage Re-weighting Mechanism (ARM) based on sampling probability dynamics analysis

key_claim: ForgetMark achieves 100% ownership verification on fingerprinted language models by encoding provenance via targeted unlearning with lightweight LoRA adapters while avoiding high-perplexity triggers.
methodology: Builds a compact human-readable key-value set using an assistant model and predictive-entropy ranking, trains LoRA adapters to suppress original values on keys, and verifies ownership via aggregated likelihood and semantic evidence (fingerprint success rate).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection affects downstream social-reasoning capabilities of language models.

key_claim: The minimum of Renyi entropy coincides with the true number of topics in labelled collections, enabling principled analysis of regularization effects on topic models.
methodology: Uses Renyi entropy inspired by statistical physics concepts to analyze regularization effects across four topic models (pLSA, BigARTM, LDA with Gibbs sampling, VLDA) and compares with HDP on labelled collections.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data provenance or authenticity of training corpora influences topic model quality or downstream social-reasoning tasks.
theoretical_framework: Renyi entropy framework applied to topic models as information statistical systems in non-equilibrium states

key_claim: Topic model output exhibits self-similar behavior under variation of cluster number, enabling renormalization combined with Renyi entropy for quick optimization of the number of topics.
methodology: Develops a renormalization procedure inspired by statistical physics for pLSA, VLDA, and GLDA, combined with Renyi entropy to efficiently search for optimal topic numbers; validated on two test datasets.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data authenticity or provenance-aware collection affects the quality of topic modeling or downstream reasoning tasks.
theoretical_framework: Renormalization group analysis combined with Renyi entropy for topic models

key_claim: A model-based pipeline with two-stage deduplication, multiclassifier quality scoring, and domain-aware fluency filtering produces a 35TB bilingual dataset that enhances LLM reasoning.
methodology: Two-stage deduplication, multiclassifier quality scoring, domain-aware fluency filtering, and staged Chain-of-Thought extraction from diverse sources rather than distillation from larger models.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the provenance or authenticity of the web-crawled sources affects downstream social-reasoning capabilities beyond general reasoning benchmarks.

key_claim: A lightweight multitask model trained on LLM-annotated Romanian texts enables multi-level filtering that improves LLM pretraining performance across benchmarks.
methodology: Training a lightweight multitask classifier on LLM-annotated Romanian texts to perform multi-level filtering (educational value, topic, format) of pretraining corpora.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether filtering by data provenance or authenticity, rather than surface quality signals, would yield further gains especially for culturally grounded social reasoning.

key_claim: Yi models' strong performance is primarily attributed to data quality from a cascaded deduplication and quality filtering pipeline over 3.1 trillion tokens.
methodology: Cascaded data deduplication and quality filtering pipeline for pretraining on 3.1T English-Chinese tokens; iterative polishing of a small (<10K) instruction-tuning dataset for finetuning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether tracking data provenance or authenticity of training sources contributes to social-reasoning performance beyond benchmark scores.

key_claim: Classifier-based quality filtering improves downstream task performance not by capturing meaningful data quality but by implicitly filtering toward the high-quality reference distribution.
methodology: Analysis of classifier-based quality filtering (CQF) by comparing language modeling loss on high-quality sets and contrasting CQF-trained models with models trained on synthetic data of varying quality via random token permutations.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether provenance-aware data selection (e.g., tracking source authenticity) would provide a more principled quality signal than classifier-based filtering for social-reasoning tasks.

key_claim: Synthetic multilingual pretraining data generated via grounded techniques at 540B-token scale can improve LLM performance for low-resource Indic languages.
methodology: Systematic generation of synthetic pretraining data using 5 techniques (document-grounded, persona-grounded, topic-grounded, translation, native generation) for 10 Indic languages, evaluated with a modular quality pipeline.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how the provenance or authenticity of the seed documents used for grounding affects downstream social-reasoning capabilities.

key_claim: Conclusions about data recipe quality from small proxy models can flip with minor hyperparameter changes, undermining standard fixed-configuration evaluation protocols.
methodology: Empirical comparison of data recipe rankings across varied training hyperparameter configurations at small scale, analyzing transferability to full-scale training.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether data provenance or authenticity signals would remain stable across proxy-model configurations, nor how social-reasoning benchmarks are affected.

key_claim: Improving HTML-to-text extraction quality via a model-based parser can be as impactful as aggressive filtering for downstream LLM performance.
methodology: A 0.6B-parameter language model (MinerU-HTML) reformulates HTML content extraction as a sequence labeling problem with a two-stage semantic formatting pipeline to produce a 7.3T-token corpus.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether preserving document provenance metadata during extraction affects data authenticity or downstream social-reasoning quality.

key_claim: Jointly optimizing data quality and diversity via a parameterized sampling function outperforms sequential quality filtering followed by proportion adjustment for LLM pretraining.
methodology: QuaDMix framework using multiple quality criteria, domain classification for diversity measurement, and a unified parameterized sampling function to automatically optimize pretraining data distribution.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address the role of data provenance or authenticity in quality measurement, nor the impact on social-reasoning outcomes.

key_claim: Sample-wise cross-domain data mixing based on per-sample quality and diversity assessments surpasses existing domain-wise pre-training data mixing methods.
methodology: Bottom-up sample-wise data mixing approach (SampleMix) that evaluates quality and diversity of each sample globally across domains to dynamically determine optimal domain distribution.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether data provenance or authenticity of training samples affects downstream social-reasoning capabilities.

key_claim: RedPajama-V1 and V2 provide open, large-scale datasets with quality signals and metadata to advance transparency and reproducibility in LLM training.
methodology: Open reproduction of the LLaMA training dataset (RedPajama-V1) and release of a massive web-only dataset with raw text plus quality signals and metadata (RedPajama-V2).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how data provenance or authenticity of collected web text influences social-reasoning or behavioral alignment outcomes in trained models.

key_claim: Fine-tuning language models on empathetic conversation data causes 10–30pp higher failure rates on safety tasks and approximately 40% more sycophancy.
methodology: Fine-tuning on empathetic conversation data followed by evaluation on safety tasks and sycophancy metrics to measure reliability trade-offs.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not compare synthetic empathy data against authentic human interaction data to isolate whether data provenance mediates the observed reliability degradation.

key_claim: Principle-driven self-alignment using LLM-generated synthetic data with minimal human supervision can produce helpful, ethical, and reliable AI assistants comparable to RLHF-based approaches.
methodology: SELF-ALIGN: four-stage pipeline using LLM-generated synthetic prompts with topic-guided diversity augmentation, a small set of human-written principles, and self-refinement to align models with minimal human supervision.
limitation: Dependence on quality and coverage of the small set of human-written principles; potential self-consistency and bias issues from synthetic data generation.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether the authenticity or provenance of alignment data (authentic human interactions vs. synthetic) affects social-reasoning quality.

key_claim: BeaverTails provides separately annotated helpfulness and harmlessness labels for 333,963 QA pairs, enabling improved safety alignment of LLMs.
methodology: Human annotation of QA pairs with separate helpfulness and harmlessness meta-labels and expert comparison data, applied to content moderation and RLHF.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance or authenticity of the annotated data affects downstream social-reasoning capabilities of aligned models.

key_claim: RRHF aligns language models with human preferences using ranking loss on conditional probabilities, avoiding PPO's complexity while requiring only 1-2 models.
methodology: Ranking loss over log conditional probabilities of sampled responses from multiple sources (self, other LLMs, human experts) to align with human preferences.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether the authenticity or provenance of training responses influences the quality of social reasoning in aligned models.

key_claim: SALMON achieves superior alignment with minimal human supervision by using an instructable reward model trained on synthetic preferences guided by human-defined principles.
methodology: Instructable reward model trained on synthetic preference data conditioned on adjustable human-defined principles, used during RL training to control alignment.
limitation: Dependency on high-quality human annotations is reduced but the approach still relies on a small set of human-defined principles whose completeness is not guaranteed.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether using authentic human-generated data rather than synthetic preferences would improve social-reasoning outcomes.

key_claim: Reinforcement learning is fundamentally a human-in-the-loop paradigm, and human-centric design is key to successful RL but has been insufficiently addressed.
methodology: Literature survey and position paper analyzing requirements, challenges, and opportunities for human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the authenticity or provenance of human feedback data specifically affects social-reasoning capabilities of trained agents.

key_claim: RLHF can be reformulated as a simple classification loss by reparameterizing the reward model, enabling direct policy optimization without reinforcement learning.
methodology: Derived a closed-form mapping between reward functions and optimal policies under the RLHF objective, replacing RL fine-tuning with a binary classification loss (DPO).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance or authenticity of preference data affects alignment quality or social-reasoning outcomes.
theoretical_framework: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) — closed-form reparameterization of the Bradley-Terry reward model within the KL-constrained RLHF objective

key_claim: RLAIF achieves comparable performance to RLHF across summarization and dialogue tasks, and direct-RLAIF outperforms canonical RLAIF by obtaining rewards directly from an LLM.
methodology: Compared RLHF (human-labeled preferences) with RLAIF (AI-labeled preferences) and direct-RLAIF (reward scoring from an off-the-shelf LLM during RL) across summarization, helpful dialogue, and harmless dialogue tasks.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether replacing human feedback with AI feedback alters the authenticity or provenance characteristics of training signal, nor its effect on social reasoning.

key_claim: RLHF is a broadly applicable technique spanning multiple domains that has been decisive in aligning LLM capabilities with human objectives.
methodology: Literature survey covering fundamentals of RLHF including preference-based reinforcement learning across multiple application domains beyond LLMs.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not analyze how the authenticity or provenance of the human feedback data affects downstream alignment quality or social-reasoning capabilities.
theoretical_framework: Preference-based reinforcement learning (PbRL) as the overarching framework for RLHF

key_claim: AI assistants finetuned with human feedback consistently exhibit sycophancy, partly because humans and preference models prefer convincingly-written sycophantic responses over correct ones.
methodology: Evaluated five state-of-the-art AI assistants on four free-form text-generation tasks for sycophantic behavior; analyzed existing human preference datasets and preference model outputs to identify preference biases favoring sycophancy.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether using provenance-aware or authenticity-verified training data could mitigate sycophancy, nor how data authenticity relates to social-reasoning fidelity.
theoretical_framework: Cognitive and computational framework linking human preference judgments to sycophantic model behavior (self-concept degradation)

key_claim: Distilled direct preference optimization from AI feedback enables a 7B model (Zephyr-7B) to surpass Llama2-Chat-70B on MT-Bench without human annotation.
methodology: Distilled supervised fine-tuning (dSFT) followed by distilled direct preference optimization (dDPO) using AI feedback preference data from a teacher model.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the provenance or authenticity of the AI-generated preference data affects the quality of social reasoning in the aligned model.

key_claim: Distribution-guided data filtering and distributional guidance via differential distribution rewards improve alignment efficiency while mitigating loss of generalization ability.
methodology: DEFT framework that calculates differential distribution rewards based on LLM output distributions and preference data discrepancy distributions to filter a high-quality data subset and guide fine-tuning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the authenticity or provenance of the underlying preference data affects the distributional filtering and downstream social-reasoning outcomes.

key_claim: FedPDPO addresses non-IID preference data heterogeneity in federated LLM alignment via personalized LoRA adapters with shared and client-specific components.
methodology: Federated personalized DPO framework using a frozen pretrained LLM backbone with LoRA adapters, combining a globally shared LoRA adapter with a personalized client-specific LLM head and personalized implicit reward modeling.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether data authenticity and provenance verification across federated clients influences the quality of learned preference alignment and social reasoning.

key_claim: Current LMs exhibit substantial misalignment with US demographic groups' opinions, on par with the Democrat-Republican divide on climate change, even after demographic steering.
methodology: Quantitative framework leveraging high-quality public opinion polls to compare LM-generated opinions against human responses across 60 US demographic groups; creation of OpinionsQA dataset.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the authenticity and provenance of post-training data (e.g., RLHF feedback sources) causally drives the observed opinion misalignment across demographic groups.
dataset_or_benchmark: Pew Research Center American Trends Panel

key_claim: LLM-simulated human feedback is 50x cheaper than crowdworkers while maintaining high agreement with actual human preferences.
methodology: Designed LLM prompts to simulate human feedback, proposed automatic evaluation validated against human judgments, and implemented reference methods (PPO, DPO, best-of-n, expert iteration) for learning from pairwise feedback.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether simulated feedback preserves the authenticity and provenance characteristics of real human preference data, potentially affecting social-reasoning outcomes.

key_claim: Process supervision significantly outperforms outcome supervision for training models to solve problems from the MATH dataset, achieving 78% accuracy.
methodology: Compared process supervision (feedback on each intermediate reasoning step) against outcome supervision (feedback on final result) for math problem solving, with active learning to improve feedback efficacy.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the provenance or authenticity of the process supervision data affects social-reasoning generalization beyond mathematical domains.

key_claim: Typicality bias in preference data is a fundamental data-level driver of mode collapse in post-training aligned LLMs.
methodology: Formalized typicality bias theoretically, verified it empirically on preference datasets, and introduced Verbalized Sampling—a training-free prompting strategy that asks the model to verbalize probability distributions over responses.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether using provenance-aware or authenticity-verified preference data (e.g., via L_auth or CampusGo-style collection) could mitigate typicality bias at the data level rather than at inference time.
theoretical_framework: Typicality bias formalization from cognitive psychology applied to preference data annotation

key_claim: Mobile phone sensing data can reveal complex social system patterns through large-scale behavioral observation.
methodology: Collected sensor data from mobile phones to detect social patterns and interactions in a community (reality mining).
what_it_does_NOT_address: How the authenticity and provenance of sensor-derived social data could inform post-training of LLMs for social reasoning.

key_claim: Smartphone sensing can continuously assess college students' mental health, academic performance, and behavioral trends in naturalistic settings.
methodology: Longitudinal smartphone sensing study collecting behavioral data (activity, sleep, sociability) from college students and correlating with mental health and academic outcomes.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address data authenticity or provenance of the behavioral signals collected, nor whether synthetic or contaminated training data would affect models built on such sensing data.

key_claim: Pre-GPT human-generated data appreciates in strategic value as AI-generated web contamination increases, analogous to low-background steel.
methodology: Legal-strategic analysis using EU competition law, essential facilities doctrine, data trustee models, and mandatory AI content labeling frameworks.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not propose a quantitative authenticity metric (such as L_auth) to measure data contamination levels or operationalize provenance-aware data collection.
theoretical_framework: Low-background steel analogy; essential facilities doctrine applied to pre-2022 uncontaminated human-generated data

key_claim: Data authenticity verification, consent tracking, and provenance preservation are systemically broken in current AI training pipelines, with ~5% of C4 tokens and 45% of content restricted by ToS.
methodology: Audit of existing data pipelines documenting failures in authenticity, consent, and provenance; empirical analysis of C4 corpus terms-of-service compliance; proposal of unified provenance standards.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not propose a formal authenticity loss function (e.g., L_auth) that quantifies downstream effects of provenance failures on social-reasoning performance of LLMs.

key_claim: A blockchain-enabled provenance framework achieves 95 percent reduction in provenance disputes and full traceability across all data contributors in AI training pipelines.
methodology: Design and evaluation of a blockchain-based provenance architecture using immutable smart contract transactions with lightweight off-chain storage, tested across three ML pipelines.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not evaluate whether provenance-verified data improves downstream model social-reasoning quality or address how data authenticity (as in L_auth) relates to post-training outcomes.
theoretical_framework: Blockchain-enabled data provenance framework with smart contract-based compliance automation

key_claim: Effective data stewardship and curation practices are essential to maintain integrity, privacy, and accessibility of vast AI-generated genomics and HTS datasets.
methodology: Systematic literature search across PubMed, MEDLINE, EMBASE, Scopus, and other scholarly platforms up to January 2024.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how data provenance or authenticity of post-training data affects social-reasoning capabilities in large language models.

key_claim: Citizen science projects perform well on data quality and governance but lack open data access, documentation, interoperability, and long-term preservation practices.
methodology: Qualitative semi-structured interviews with 36 purposively sampled citizen science project proponents using landscape sampling, analyzed with descriptive statistics.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how data provenance practices in citizen science affect downstream model training authenticity or social-reasoning performance.

key_claim: Stewarding volunteer-generated citizen science data requires ethical decisions about power, attribution, access, and data governance beyond standard technical considerations.
methodology: Perspective essay advancing normative propositions about ethical dimensions of open data in citizen science based on analysis of professional-volunteer power dynamics.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how ethical data provenance and volunteer attribution affect data authenticity for training AI models with social-reasoning capabilities.

key_claim: FAIR-guided data harmonization involves inherent trade-offs between standardization and information loss when sharing individual participant data for pooled biomedical analyses.
methodology: Conceptual analysis of FAIR data governance principles applied to individual participant data harmonization and pooling for meta-analysis.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how harmonization and provenance tracking of training data influence the social-reasoning performance of post-trained language models.
theoretical_framework: FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) data governance principles

key_claim: An open-access platform with version control and community governance can resolve fragmented provenance in mental health outcome measures.
methodology: Technical specification proposing version control, adaptation management, and governance standards for mental health outcome measures.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how provenance-aware data collection practices affect downstream model training or social-reasoning outcomes in AI systems.

key_claim: The CARE principles provide a framework for Indigenous data governance that redresses hidden costs borne by Indigenous communities in archaeological data sharing and reuse.
methodology: Conceptual analysis applying the CARE (Collective benefit, Authority to control, Responsibility, Ethics) principles to archaeological data practices.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how Indigenous data governance or provenance-aware collection affects AI training data authenticity or social-reasoning model outcomes.
theoretical_framework: CARE Principles (Collective benefit, Authority to control, Responsibility, Ethics)

key_claim: Data integrity of NHS Digital datasets can be demonstrated by documenting data lifecycles and assessing accuracy, reliability, completeness, and contemporaneity.
methodology: Mapping data lifecycles from healthcare providers through NHS Digital warehouses to clinical trial teams, evaluating provenance, quality, and reliability.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether provenance documentation of healthcare datasets influences downstream AI model training quality or social-reasoning capabilities.

key_claim: Properly filtered and deduplicated web data alone can train language models that significantly outperform models trained on curated corpora like The Pile.
methodology: Extensive filtering and deduplication of CommonCrawl web data to create the RefinedWeb dataset, training 1.3B and 7.5B parameter language models and benchmarking against state-of-the-art models.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the authenticity or provenance of the web data sources affects social-reasoning capabilities of the resulting models.

key_claim: FineWeb, a 15-trillion token dataset from 96 Common Crawl snapshots, produces better-performing LLMs than other open pretraining datasets, and FineWeb-Edu dramatically improves knowledge- and reasoning-intensive benchmarks.
methodology: Systematic curation of Common Crawl data with documented ablations of deduplication and filtering strategies; creation of an educational text subset (FineWeb-Edu) via quality filtering.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address how the provenance or authenticity of the web-crawled data affects downstream social-reasoning capabilities of models trained on it.

key_claim: The Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus (C4) contains significant unexpected content including machine-generated text and benchmark evaluation examples, and its blocklist filtering disproportionately removes text from certain populations.
methodology: Documentation and audit of C4 through provenance analysis of source domains, content inspection for machine-generated text and benchmark leakage, and evaluation of the effects of applied filters on demographic representation.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the documented data quality and provenance issues in C4 specifically affect models' social-reasoning or theory-of-mind performance.

key_claim: Over 5% of newly created English Wikipedia articles are flagged as AI-generated, and these articles are typically of lower quality and often self-promotional or biased.
methodology: Application of GPTZero (proprietary) and Binoculars (open-source) AI detectors to Wikipedia articles, with detection thresholds calibrated to a 1% false positive rate on pre-GPT-3.5 articles, comparing AI content prevalence across time periods and languages.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how the infiltration of AI-generated content into Wikipedia affects the authenticity and provenance of downstream training data used for social-reasoning tasks in LLMs.

key_claim: A curated longitudinal dataset of over one million English privacy policies spanning two decades reveals how the privacy policy landscape changed in response to evolving regulations such as GDPR.
methodology: Development of a crawler to discover, download, and extract archived privacy policies from the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine, combined with NLP-based curation and longitudinal analysis of policy content and regulatory compliance.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance and authenticity of web-crawled policy text could affect LLM training data quality or social-reasoning outcomes.

key_claim: In 2023-2024, web data restrictions grew rapidly, rendering over 5% of tokens in major AI corpora like C4 nonconsented due to ineffective consent protocols.
methodology: Large-scale longitudinal audit of consent protocols (robots.txt and Terms of Service) across 14,000 web domains underlying AI training corpora.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether training on consent-restricted versus unrestricted web data affects downstream model quality or social-reasoning capabilities.

key_claim: A small number of dominant companies capture the majority of online attention in each functional market segment, consistent with preferential attachment dynamics.
methodology: Large-scale longitudinal analysis of external link volumes in two social media datasets (10+ billion posts over a decade) and the Common Crawl dataset as proxies for online attention.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how corporate dominance in web content affects the provenance or authenticity composition of AI training corpora.
theoretical_framework: preferential attachment and increasing returns economics

key_claim: Analyzing Wikipedia edit histories enables effective extraction of complex, evolving event structures generalizable across languages.
methodology: Evolution-aware entity-based enrichment algorithms applied to Wikipedia user edit records, with web-based automatic document assessment for quality improvement.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance or authenticity of collaboratively edited data like Wikipedia affects downstream AI social-reasoning performance.

key_claim: Privacy policies have evolved over 25 years with measurable content changes in response to regulations like GDPR and CCPA, though gaps remain in user rights.
methodology: Machine learning and NLP analysis of a large-scale longitudinal corpus of privacy policies from 1996 to 2021, examining data practices, user rights, and organizational rights.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not assess how evolving privacy policy restrictions affect the authenticity or composition of data available for AI training corpora.

key_claim: Quantitative analysis of 25+ years of web evolution shows streaming media and social networking sites have replaced news and education websites in popularity.
methodology: Longitudinal analysis of top 100 Alexa websites over 25+ years using snapshots from the Internet Archive (Wayback Machine)
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the changing composition of web content affects the quality or authenticity of data used for training language models.

key_claim: Instruction tuning partially helps Theory of Mind in LLMs, but reasoning-oriented fine-tuning amplifies problematic response patterns on False Belief tasks.
methodology: Evaluation of 17 language models on 192 False Belief task variants, tracking pre-training learning traces across model sizes and fine-tuning strategies
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether using authentic human social-reasoning data during post-training could mitigate the problematic patterns introduced by reasoning-oriented fine-tuning.

key_claim: Even LLMs fine-tuned on 60K human participants fail to replicate human behavior distributions on the 11-20 money request game, showing synthetic surrogacy is unreliable.
methodology: Testing advanced LLM techniques (few-shot CoT, RAG, fine-tuning including the Centaur model fine-tuned on 60K participants) against human behavior distributions on the 11-20 money request game
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether provenance-aware data collection (tracking authenticity and source diversity) could improve fine-tuned model alignment with human cognitive complexity.

key_claim: Fine-tuning on 2.9M authentic human behavioral responses makes a 14B-parameter model 36% more aligned with human distributions than the base model and 15% better than GPT-4o.
methodology: Fine-tuning Qwen-14B (Socrates-Qwen-14B) on the SocSci210 dataset of 2.9M responses from 400,491 participants across 210 social science experiments, comparing against base model and GPT-4o
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether data provenance metadata (e.g., participant demographics, experimental context tracking) further improves alignment beyond volume of authentic data alone.

key_claim: LLM behavioral simulation studies require explicit model selection justification, replicability protocols, prompt documentation, and output validity checks to avoid training data contamination.
methodology: Methodological checklist and best-practice guidelines for validating LLM use in social-science research
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not empirically test whether provenance-aware post-training data (L_auth) improves social-reasoning outcomes compared to standard web-scraped data.

key_claim: Pre-training, SFT, and preference optimization stages differentially affect pragmatic competence in LLMs, as measured by the ALTPRAG benchmark across 22 models.
methodology: Stage-specific evaluation of 22 LLMs on the ALTPRAG benchmark assessing pragmatic abilities across pre-training, SFT, and RLHF stages
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the authenticity or provenance of post-training data (rather than the training stage itself) drives observed differences in social-pragmatic competence.

key_claim: A foundation model fine-tuned on Psych-101 (10M+ choices from 60K+ participants across 160 experiments) predicts held-out human behavior better than existing cognitive models.
methodology: Fine-tuning a large language model on the Psych-101 large-scale behavioral dataset and evaluating prediction of held-out human choices against existing cognitive models
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not disentangle whether improvements stem from data authenticity and provenance versus sheer data scale, leaving the L_auth mechanism unexamined.

key_claim: Prompt tuning with extracted human sentiment, emotion, and intention signals improves LLM Theory of Mind reasoning by 29–43% on open-ended responses.
methodology: Prompt tuning that injects human mental-state signals (sentiment, emotion, intention) extracted from naturalistic Reddit ChangeMyView data to enhance LLM ToM reasoning
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether systematically collecting provenance-verified authentic interaction data (as in CampusGo) would yield further gains beyond ad hoc behavioral signal extraction.

key_claim: Social IQa provides a large-scale benchmark for evaluating commonsense reasoning about people's actions, intents, and reactions in social interactions.
methodology: Construction of a question-answering benchmark focused on social commonsense reasoning about everyday interactions
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance or authenticity of training data affects model performance on social reasoning tasks

key_claim: GPT-3 lacks social intelligence and Theory of Mind out-of-the-box, struggling substantially on SocialIQa and ToMi benchmarks.
methodology: Empirical evaluation of GPT-3 on two social reasoning tasks (SocialIQa and ToMi) combined with theory-based analysis of social intelligence limitations
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection could improve social reasoning capabilities

key_claim: LLMs possess internal ToM knowledge but fail to externalize it reliably; causal-oriented steering can align internal cognition with stable external ToM behavior.
methodology: Causal tracing to map internal ToM feature distributions followed by lightweight alignment intervention (CoSToM framework) transitioning from mechanistic interpretation to active steering
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the authenticity or provenance of post-training data influences the formation of internal ToM representations

key_claim: CoMMET is the first multimodal multi-turn benchmark evaluating LLMs on a broad range of mental states and moral reasoning beyond belief-only ToM tasks.
methodology: Construction of a multimodal benchmark dataset inspired by the Theory of Mind Booklet Task, with multi-turn conversational evaluation across multiple LLM families and sizes
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether training data authenticity or provenance-aware data curation affects LLM performance on ToM tasks

key_claim: Reinforcement learning with multi-dimensional process rewards on adversarially hard social reasoning examples yields more human-like social intelligence in LLMs than outcome-based RL.
methodology: Reinforcement learning framework (Social-R1) with multi-dimensional rewards supervising the entire reasoning process, trained on adversarial hard examples from ToMBench-Hard.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the provenance and authenticity of the social-reasoning training data (e.g., human-generated vs. synthetic scenarios) affects the quality of learned social cognition.

key_claim: Language models can be aligned to follow instructions using self-generated instruction data, reducing reliance on costly human annotations.
methodology: Self-Instruct: iterative bootstrapping of instruction-following examples generated by the model itself, used for fine-tuning.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the synthetic (self-generated) nature of instructions degrades social reasoning quality compared to authentic human-authored instructions.

key_claim: A large-scale crowd-sourced, human-annotated conversation corpus enables open and democratized research on LLM alignment.
methodology: Worldwide crowd-sourcing effort to collect and annotate 161,443 messages across 35 languages with 461,292 quality ratings, yielding over 10,000 fully annotated conversation trees.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the provenance metadata of crowd-sourced contributors affects downstream social-reasoning alignment quality.

key_claim: Fine-tuning a 65B LLM on only 1,000 carefully curated examples without reinforcement learning achieves performance comparable or preferred to GPT-4 in 43% of cases.
methodology: Supervised fine-tuning of LLaMA 65B on 1,000 manually curated prompt-response pairs, evaluated via controlled human studies.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether data authenticity and provenance of the curated 1,000 examples (e.g., human-written vs. model-generated) is the key driver of alignment quality rather than sheer curation effort.

key_claim: LLM-rewritten instructions via Evol-Instruct produce higher-complexity training data that yields a model preferred over ChatGPT on complex tasks.
methodology: Evol-Instruct iteratively rewrites seed instructions into more complex variants using an LLM; mixed-complexity data is used to fine-tune LLaMA (WizardLM).
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether synthetically evolved instructions preserve the authenticity or provenance characteristics of real human data, and how this affects social-reasoning or Theory-of-Mind capabilities.

key_claim: Self-guided data selection for instruction tuning can boost LLM performance by prioritizing data quality over quantity.
methodology: Self-guided data selection method that filters instruction-tuning data to retain high-quality subsets.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether data selection strategies account for provenance or authenticity of training data and their downstream effects on social reasoning.

key_claim: Hybrid training with both real and synthetic data consistently outperforms real-only and synthetic-only fine-tuning in therapy/counseling domains.
methodology: Three-arm comparison (base, real-only, hybrid real+synthetic) fine-tuning of LLMs in a social/emotional therapy-counseling domain.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the hybrid advantage extends beyond counseling response quality to genuine Theory-of-Mind or behavioral prediction, and whether provenance metadata (L_auth) mediates the observed gains.

key_claim: A synthetic empathy data pipeline with quality and diversity selection achieves state-of-the-art empathetic response performance.
methodology: Synthetic data generation pipeline incorporating quality filtering and diversity selection modules for empathetic dialogue.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether synthetic empathy data success in response quality transfers to genuine Theory-of-Mind reasoning or behavioral prediction, leaving the role of data authenticity (L_auth) unexamined.

key_claim: The example component of in-context alignment is crucial for enhancing model alignment, with changes in examples significantly affecting alignment performance.
methodology: Ablation experiments decomposing ICA context into format, system prompt, and example components, followed by zero-shot evaluation across alignment tasks.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the provenance or authenticity of the in-context examples affects alignment quality compared to synthetic demonstrations.

key_claim: Fine-tuning dialogical language models with human post-edited data yields higher perceived quality outputs compared to using unedited machine-generated dialogues.
methodology: Created HED-IT dataset pairing machine-generated dialogues with human post-edited versions; fine-tuned three different sizes of an LM on edited vs. unedited data and compared outputs.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate how post-editing provenance metadata or traceability of human edits could systematically inform data curation beyond quality improvement.

key_claim: Fine-tuning dialogical language models with human post-edited data yields higher perceived quality outputs compared to using unedited machine-generated dialogues.
methodology: Created HED-IT dataset pairing machine-generated dialogues with human post-edited versions; fine-tuned three different sizes of an LM on edited vs. unedited data and compared outputs.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether provenance-aware data collection (e.g., tracking edit history and editor expertise) could further improve data authenticity effects on social reasoning.

key_claim: Scaling diverse, high-quality synthetic instructional conversations (UltraChat) without human queries can substantially improve open-source chat language model performance.
methodology: Systematically designed a comprehensive framework to iteratively generate multi-turn conversations via API-driven LLM interactions, then fine-tuned models on the resulting UltraChat dataset.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether the lack of authentic human-generated queries undermines social reasoning or alignment quality compared to provenance-verified human data.

key_claim: Filtering and labeling conversational data enhances efficiency of both internal fine-tuning and external user analytics applications of LLMs.
methodology: Literature review of internal (fine-tuning, RLHF, multi-turn dialogue SFT) and external (user analytics, behavior prediction) applications of LLM conversational data
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine how the provenance or authenticity of conversational training data affects social-reasoning capabilities of LLMs.

key_claim: DEL-ToM improves Theory-of-Mind reasoning in small language models through inference-time scaling using a Dynamic Epistemic Logic-grounded verifier rather than architectural changes.
methodology: Decomposes ToM tasks into belief update sequences grounded in DEL; trains a Process Belief Model (PBM) verifier on automatically generated DEL simulator labels to score candidate belief traces at inference time.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection could complement or substitute for inference-time scaling in improving social reasoning.

key_claim: Self-consistency decoding, which marginalizes over diverse sampled reasoning paths, substantially boosts chain-of-thought prompting performance on arithmetic and commonsense reasoning benchmarks.
methodology: Samples multiple reasoning paths from a language model and selects the most consistent final answer via majority voting, replacing greedy decoding in chain-of-thought prompting.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not examine whether self-consistency improves social or Theory-of-Mind reasoning, nor whether training data authenticity influences the quality of sampled reasoning paths.

key_claim: Frontier VLMs achieve high overall ToM accuracy (>93%) on cross-cultural probes but still fail on false belief reasoning and exhibit systematic social desirability bias.
methodology: Constructed CulturalToM-VQA benchmark via frontier MLLM generation with human verification; benchmarked 10 VLMs across six ToM task types and four complexity levels with ablation experiments.
limitation: Models struggle with false belief reasoning (19-83% accuracy), show high regional variance (20-30% gaps), and exhibit social desirability bias favoring positive answer choices.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not investigate whether post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data curation could mitigate cultural biases and social desirability bias in VLM ToM reasoning.

key_claim: SymbCoT, integrating symbolic expressions and logic rules with chain-of-thought prompting, consistently and significantly outperforms standard CoT on logical reasoning benchmarks.
methodology: Three-stage LLM pipeline: natural language to symbolic translation, step-by-step symbolic reasoning plan derivation, and a verifier for translation and reasoning chain correctness.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether improvements stem from the structured symbolic representation itself or from increased inference-time computation, and how post-training data authenticity may affect symbolic translation fidelity.
dataset_or_benchmark: 5 standard logical reasoning datasets (First-Order Logic and Constraint Optimization)

key_claim: PRIOR-PQ, a probabilistic ToM model grounding question-answering pragmatics in questioner goal inference, predicts human overinformative answering patterns better than LLMs.
methodology: Probabilistic cognitive model (PRIOR-PQ) formalizing theory-of-mind reasoning about questioner goals, evaluated against human answering behavior in three case studies of increasing complexity.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether LLM social reasoning in question answering could be improved by training on authentic pragmatic interaction data rather than through inference-time prompting strategies.
dataset_or_benchmark: Three human behavioral case studies (polar question answering)
theoretical_framework: Rational Speech Act (RSA) / Bayesian pragmatics extended with goal inference (PRIOR-PQ)

key_claim: PRIOR-PQ, a goal-sensitive probabilistic ToM model, predicts nuanced human relevance judgments in polar question answering better than state-of-the-art LLMs.
methodology: Probabilistic cognitive model of theory-of-mind-based pragmatic reasoning about questioner goals, evaluated against human behavior in three case studies; also proposes a novel chain-of-thought prompting method for LLMs.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the gap between LLMs and human pragmatic reasoning reflects deficiencies in training data authenticity rather than architectural or prompting limitations.
dataset_or_benchmark: Three human behavioral case studies (polar question answering)
theoretical_framework: Bayesian pragmatics / goal-sensitive Rational Speech Act model (PRIOR-PQ)

key_claim: SymbCoT, integrating symbolic expressions and logic rules with chain-of-thought prompting, consistently and significantly outperforms standard CoT on logical reasoning benchmarks.
methodology: Three-stage LLM framework: (1) translate natural language to symbolic format, (2) derive step-by-step plan using symbolic logical rules, (3) verify translation and reasoning chain.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Whether the quality or provenance of post-training data affects the LLM's ability to faithfully perform symbolic translation and logical deduction.
dataset_or_benchmark: 5 standard datasets (First-Order Logic and Constraint Optimization)

key_claim: Aham combines symbolic meta-reasoning with latent state steering to enable real-time self-regulated Theory-of-Mind sensitivity in large language models.
methodology: Modular cognitive architecture with a meta-reasoning Arbiter that intercepts chain-of-thought traces and modulates behavior via explicit CoT rewriting and Latent State Steering (LSS) injecting ToM-derived vectors into the model's residual stream using Dynamic Residual Injection.
what_it_does_NOT_address: Does not address whether post-training data authenticity or provenance-aware data collection influences the quality of social reasoning or Theory-of-Mind outcomes independently of architectural interventions.
theoretical_framework: Aham metacognitive architecture with Latent State Steering and Dynamic Residual Injection
